content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441

April 9, 1984

Marcia Karp
2112 Lakeview Drive
San Leandro, CA 94577

Re: Advice Letter No. A-84-073

Dear Mrs. Karp:

This letter is written in response to your questions concerning the disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act.

FACTS

Your husband is a San Leandro City Councilman. He owns 50% of Soap and Chemical Co. in Oakland, California. He has several clients in San Leandro, each of whom provide the business with less than $500 in income. You also indicated that, during the reporting period, you and your husband sold a car to a Fremont resident.

QUESTION #1

How should your husband report his investment interest in Soap and Chemical Co.?

ANSWER #1

Your husband should report his investment interest in Soap and Chemical Co. on Schedule A - Investments. He should indicate that he has a 10% or greater interest and state the value of the interest. On Schedule D - Income, he should report his pro rata share (50%) of the company's gross income. This reporting is required because Soap and Chemical Co. does business in your husband's jurisdiction. He will not be required to report his San Leandro clients on Schedule H - Income to Business Entities because they do not pay the company sufficient income. To be reportable on Schedule H, a client would have to provide your husband's company with $20,000 or more in gross income.

Marcia Karp
April 9, 1984
Page 2


QUESTION #2

Is your husband required to report the purchaser of the
automobile on Schedule D - Income?

ANSWER #2

The purchaser of the automobile is not reportable because he
is a Fremont resident and is not in your husband's jurisdiction.
(Gov. Code Section 82030(a).)

If you have any additional questions, please feel free to
contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Very truly yours,

Janis Shank McLean
Janis Shank McLean
Counsel
Legal Division

JSM:plh
cc: Richard West, San Leandro City Clerk

March 22, 1984

Mrs. David Karp
2112 Lakeview Drive
San Leandro, California 94577
(415) 483-1259

Ms Carla Wardlow
c/o Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Ms Wardlow:

As per our conversation on Wed. March 21, 1984, I am requesting
an official ruling on two points in regards to the Statement Of
Economic Interests, Form 721.

My husband is a councilmember in San Leandro and owns a 50%
interest in a Soap & Chemical Co. in Oakland, California. He
has several accounts in San Leandro that he sells to, each one
less than $500, for a total amount of less than $1,000.

You instructed me to have him fill out Schedule A-Investments
for his company because he does business in San Leandro and to
report his ownership interest as greater than 10% and his total
invested interest value in the business. You also said to have
him fill out Schedule D-Income, for his total amount of income
from the business. My understanding was that he is not to re-
port the names, addresses or amounts of the seperate accounts
he sells in the city.

Another issue I discussed with you was the fact that we sold a
car to a Fremont, California resident. You stated that since
the buyer was not in his jurisdiction he needn't file the
income under Schedule D.

Please send an official ruling because the information you gave
was at odds with the information given by the City Clerk of San
Leandro. Many thanks for your time.

Yours truly,

Marcia Karp

Marcia Karp

sections.conclusion: ANSWER #1: Your husband should report his investment interest in Soap and Chemical Co. on Schedule A - Investments, indicating a 10% or greater interest and stating the value. On Schedule D - Income, he should report his pro rata share (50%) of the company's gross income because the company does business in his jurisdiction. San Leandro clients need not be reported on Schedule H because they do not provide sufficient income ($20,000 or more threshold). ANSWER #2: The purchaser of the automobile is not reportable because he is a Fremont resident and is not in your husband's jurisdiction.
sections.facts:
Your husband is a San Leandro City Councilman. 
He owns 50% 
of Soap and Chemical Co. in Oakland, California. 
He has several 
clients in San Leandro, each of whom provide the business with 
less than $500 in income. 
You also indicated that, during the 
reporting period, you and your husband sold a car to a Fremont 
resident.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asked how to report her husband's 50% ownership interest in Soap and Chemical Co. on the Statement of Economic Interests form, and whether the sale of a car to a Fremont resident must be reported on Schedule D.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The husband must report his 50% investment interest and pro rata income share from Soap and Chemical Co. on Schedules A and D because the company does business in his jurisdiction, but need not report individual San Leandro clients or the car sale to a non-resident on Schedule D.
sections.parsing_notes: Mixed formats: modern, ocr; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request letter (dated March 22, 1984) and the FPPC response letter (dated April 9, 1984). The response is clearly structured with Facts, Question #1-#2 and Answer #1-#2 sections. Minor OCR inconsistencies noted (e.g., 'Executi"/legai' instead of 'Executive/Legal'). Extraction confidence is high as the Q&A structure is clear.

embedding.summary: FPPC Advice Letter A-84-073 responds to questions about disclosure requirements for a San Leandro City Councilman's investment in a business and asset sales, providing guidance on which schedules require reporting based on jurisdictional and income threshold requirements.
